meaning: v.鏁茶瘓,閫煎彇,寮哄彇,鍕掔储
examples: Both extort large sumsof money from mines they control.
You shouldn't go after a journalist just because they bought stolen property and tried to extort you.
'you shouldn't go after a journalist because they bought stolen property and tried to extort you.'
This narrative holds that patent trolls extort virtuous companies via spurious patent infringement claims.
At least one was accused by colleagues of trying to extort money by threatening negative coverage.
